1.1: Philemon was the master of a slave named Onesimus, who, having run away from his home, took refuge with Saint Paul. The Apostle converted him, reconciled him with Philemon, and made him an apostle.
1.2: Appia was very likely, as Saint John Chrysostom supposed, the wife of Philemon. Archippe must have been their son. To the church that is in your house . Philemon's house served as a church or meeting place for the faithful.
1.4: Constantly remembering you . See, for the true meaning of this expression, Romans ,
1.5: To all the saints . See Acts of the Apostles ,
1.13: The chains I wear for the gospel ; that is to say, the bonds with which I am charged for having proclaimed the Gospel.
1.16: According to the flesh , from a social point of view, being your slave; according to the Lord , in his capacity as a Christian.
1.18: Onesimus had caused serious harm to his master simply by running away; perhaps he had also committed some theft.
1.24: Marc , see Acts of the Apostles ,
1.12: . ― Aristarchus . See Acts of the Apostles ,
